rules.heading: Status and source
rules.summary: This compact file is generated from the current KIN contract. It is non-normative, must not be edited, and does not replace the linked source documents.
do: Use it to select a mode, product profile, and first-read constraints before opening the detailed contract.
do_not: Do not treat generated guidance, a passing check, or a themed component lab as proof of product adoption.

labels.title: KIN Agent design snapshot
labels.generated_warning: Generated derivative. Read the complete contract when changing normative behavior or delivery boundaries.
labels.publication_notice: public locators are reserved and are not live in Phase 1
labels.color_scheme_guidance: Set color-scheme to the resolved theme and preserve light, dark, system, and higher-contrast behavior in the product.

rules.heading: Visual register
rules.summary: Put the subject, current task, search, list, document, object, or action in the first meaningful view.
do: Let real content and the user's job establish the composition.
do_not: Do not place a marketing Hero, generic value proposition, or decorative graph before the working task.

rules.heading: Visual register
rules.summary: Give each state one dominant work or reading region; navigation, metadata, and secondary chrome recede after orientation.
do: Use visual weight to make the next useful action obvious.
do_not: Do not give unrelated panels equal weight merely because a grid is available.

rules.heading: Theme usage
rules.summary: Keep most structure neutral and use one primary interaction accent for focus, selection, links, and primary actions.
do: Keep semantic colors tied to distinct product meanings.
do_not: Do not use color, badges, icons, or motion to compensate for weak order or alignment.

rules.heading: Typography roles
rules.summary: Use typography roles to express hierarchy; keep interface text compact and body text readable, and use tabular numerals where comparison matters.
do: Map the named roles to the product's available font stack without redistributing private fonts.
do_not: Do not enlarge headings or tighten Chinese tracking merely to imitate another product.

rules.heading: Layout and density
rules.summary: Align repeated values and actions, carry shared meaning in columns or group controls, and use empty space to clarify priority.
do: Keep comparison rows compact while preserving required hit areas.
do_not: Do not repeat labels and helper text in every row or use large empty regions as unresolved composition.

rules.heading: Surface and elevation
rules.summary: Create hierarchy with alignment, rhythm, columns, property rows, dividers, and small surface steps before adding containers.
do: Use a Surface only for a real object, overlay, selected context, or task-mode boundary.
do_not: Do not turn prose, metadata, activity, property groups, filters, or table regions into nested cards by default.

rules.heading: Motion
rules.summary: Use motion to preserve origin, direction, state continuity, and committed results; reversible interactions begin from their current visual state.
do: Keep motion interruptible and preserve the same final hierarchy under Reduced Motion.
do_not: Do not use repeated lifts, automatic counting, generic page fades, or decorative entrances to manufacture polish.

rules.heading: Content rules
rules.summary: Keep status, risk, confidence, completeness, availability, permission, and progress separate whenever the product distinguishes them.
do: Make meaning readable with language or symbols as well as color, and show source and time for consequential data.
do_not: Do not collapse independent judgments into one attractive score, badge, or unsupported claim.

rules.heading: Task and product-profile routing
rules.summary: Choose the product profile from the real route and task, then open the matching detailed pattern before changing composition.
do: Use route-level profiles for hybrid products and preserve the user's stated request and product truth.
do_not: Do not apply one workspace layout to every route or let task-intent hints override local requirements.

rules.heading: Verification and adoption boundary
rules.summary: Generated snapshots, Token parity, builds, and component references are delivery evidence, not proof that a product is visibly or fully KIN.
do: Record scoped mappings, realistic states, comparable screenshots, human review, exceptions, owners, and rollback before making an adoption claim.
do_not: Do not infer production adoption from this snapshot, a passing generator, or isolated styled components.
